    21. getErrors()

Interface Validation<E,T>

Type Parameters:
E - the error element/container type
T - the validated type
All Known Subinterfaces:
Invalid<E,T>
All Known Implementing Classes:
Accumulated, Critical, Valid
public sealed interface Validation<E,T> permits Valid<E,T>, Invalid<E,T>
A disjoint union (sum type) representing one of three distinct states:
  • Valid<T>: A validated value.
  • Accumulated<E>: A List<E> of accumulated validation errors.
  • Critical<E>: A single major error that short circuits the validation process.
Validation features:
  • Is null safe: Valid or Invalid can never contain a null value, Validation does not expose any null value entry or exit. The entire API rejects null at every boundary: constructors, mapping functions, and suppliers all throw NullPointerException on null.
  • An applicative functor: combine(Validation) joins multiple independent validations and accumulates all errors.
  • A monad (right biased): then(CheckedFunction) transforms the contained target into a new Validation when this is Valid.
  • A functor (right biased): mapTarget(CheckedFunction) transforms the contained target when this is Valid.

Note:
Accumulated and Critical are subtypes of Invalid, therefore Validation can be reasoned with as a Valid XOR Invalid. However, the two Invalid subtypes behave differently, see Invalid.combineInvalid(Invalid).

Exception Handling:
Methods accepting a CheckedFunction or CheckedSupplier propagate checked exceptions transparently through a generic X extends Exception parameter. The compiler will only require handling if checked exceptions are explicitly declared or thrown in the lambda body. Unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException) propagate normally and require no declaration. Example of checked exception handling: 

    try {
        Validation.valid(7).then(n -> {
            if (n < 10) throw new Exception();
            return Validation.valid(n);
        });
    } catch (Exception e) {
        // e has to be handled as it propagated from then
    }
While throwing unchecked exceptions does not require explicit handling: 
    Validation.valid(7).then(n -> {
        if (n < 10) throw new RuntimeException();
        return Validation.valid(n);
    });
Alternative Types:
For sequential, non-accumulative error handling, use Either.
For single value presence or absence handling, use Option.
Since:
1.0.0
Author:
Igor Flakiewicz
See Also:
  • Valid
  • Invalid
  • Accumulated
  • Critical

  • Method Details

    • valid

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> valid(T target)
      Factory method for creating a new Valid<E, T> instance. Containing the validation target T.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      target - The validation target.
      Returns:
      A new Valid<E, T> instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If value is null.
      See Also:
      • Valid

    • accumulated

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> accumulated(E error)
      Factory method for creating a new Accumulated<E, T> instance. Containing the validation error E.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      error - The validation error information.
      Returns:
      A new Accumulated<E, T> instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If error is null.
      See Also:
      • Accumulated

    • accumulated

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> accumulated(List<E> errors) throws EmptyValueException
      Factory method for creating a new Accumulated<E, T> instance. Containing the validation errors List<E>.

      EmptyValueException is thrown when the provided errors is empty. A Accumulated.errors() can never contain null or empty values. A checked exception is an intentional design choice to force the user handle empty cases at call point.

      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      errors - The validation errors information.
      Returns:
      A new Accumulated<E, T> instance.
      Throws:
      EmptyValueException - If errors is empty.
      NullPointerException - If errors is null.
      See Also:
      • Accumulated

    • critical

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> critical(E error)
      Factory method for creating a new Critical<E, T> instance. Containing the critical validation error E.

      Critical is a short circuit that bypasses any further error accumulation.

      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      error - The validation error information.
      Returns:
      A new Critical<E, T> instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If error is null.
      See Also:
      • Critical

    • validate

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> validate(T value, Predicate<? super T> predicate, Function<? super T, ? extends E> errorMapper, boolean critical)
      A convenience factory method for creating a Validation<E, T> based on the result of value being tested against predicate.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to be validated.
      predicate - The condition tested against value.
      errorMapper - Error provider should the result be Invalid.
      critical - If true then an Invalid result will be of type Critical<E, T>. If false the result type will be Accumulated<E, T>.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, T>, containing value, when predicate returns true.
      Accumulated<E, T>, containing result of errorMapper, when predicate returns false and critical is false.
      Critical<E, T>, containing result of errorMapper, when predicate returns false and critical is true.
      See Also:
      • Valid
      • Accumulated
      • Critical

    • validate

      static <E,T> Validation<E,T> validate(T value, Predicate<? super T> predicate, Function<? super T, ? extends E> errorMapper)
      A convenience factory method overload for validate(Object, Predicate, Function, boolean).

      This method always assumes that any error is Accumulated.

      Type Parameters:
      E - The error type reference.
      T - The type of the object being validated.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to be validated.
      predicate - The condition tested against value.
      errorMapper - Error provider should the result be Accumulated.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, T>, containing value, when predicate returns true.
      Accumulated<E, T>, containing result of errorMapper, when predicate returns false.
      See Also:
      • Valid
      • Accumulated
      • validate(Object, Predicate, Function, boolean)

    • combine

      <S, U, X extends Exception> Validation<E,U> combine(Validation<E,S> other, CheckedBiFunction<? super T, ? super S, ? extends U, ? extends X> combiner) throws X
      The applicative functor operation of Validation. Combines this with some other validation Validation<E, S>.

      Scenarios:

      • When this is a Valid and other is Valid, uses the combiner to map this contained target and the other contained target, producing a new Valid<E, U>.
      • When this is a Valid and other is some Invalid, returns other as Validation<E, U>. Preserving the contained errors, and binding to the new target type U.
      • When this is a Accumulated and other is Valid, returns this as Validation<E, U>, binding to the new target type U.
      • When this is a Accumulated and other is Accumulated, combines errors from this and other into a new Accumulated<E, U>. Preserving the contained errors, and binding to the new target type U.
      • When this is a Accumulated and other is Critical, returns other as Validation<E, U>. Preserving the contained errors, and binding to the new target type U.
      • When this is a Critical, ignores other, returns this as Validation<E, U>. Preserving the contained error, and binding to the new target type U.

      The combiner doesn't have to be concerned with handling null contained values as Valid.target() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by combiner are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      S - The type of object validated by other.
      U - The target type of the returned Validation.
      Parameters:
      other - Validation to combine with this.
      combiner - Used to map this contained target and the other contained target, when both are Valid.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, U>, Accumulated<E, U>, or Critical<E, U>. See scenarios above for details.
      Throws:
      X - If the combiner throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If other is null. Or, combiner is null or returns null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.combine(Validation, CheckedBiFunction)
      • Invalid.combine(Validation, CheckedBiFunction)

    • combine

      <S> Validation<E,T> combine(Validation<E,S> other)
      The applicative functor operation of Validation. Similar to combine(Validation, CheckedBiFunction).

      Unlike combine(Validation, CheckedBiFunction), this method preserves the contained target T in this, when this and other are both Valid.

      Type Parameters:
      S - The type of object validated by other.
      Parameters:
      other - Validation to combine with this.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, T>, Accumulated<E, T>, or Critical<E, T>. See description above for details.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If other is null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.combine(Validation)
      • Invalid.combine(Validation)
      • combine(Validation, CheckedBiFunction)

    • then

      <S, X extends Exception> Validation<E,S> then(CheckedFunction<? super T, ? extends Validation<E,S>, ? extends X> validator) throws X
      The monad (right biased) operation of Validation. validator is only executed when this is Valid, allowing for lazy evaluation of dependent validations.

      The validator doesn't have to be concerned with handling null contained values as Valid.target() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by validator are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      S - The target type of the returned Validation.
      Parameters:
      validator - Used to supply a Validation<E, S> when this is Valid.
      Returns:
      Validation<E, S> produced by validator when this is Valid. Otherwise, returns this as Validation<E, S>, preserving errors and binding to the new target type S
      Throws:
      X - If the validator throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If validator is null or returns null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.then(CheckedFunction)
      • Invalid.then(CheckedFunction)

    • mapError

      <U, X extends Exception> Validation<U,T> mapError(CheckedFunction<? super E, ? extends U, ? extends X> errorMapper) throws X
      Allows item by item conversion of any contained errors from type E to type U, when this is Invalid. When this is Valid the conversion is not applied as there are no errors, but the new error type binding U is preserved.

      The errorMapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null or empty errors as Accumulated.errors() and Critical.error() can never be null or empty.

      Checked exceptions thrown by errorMapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      U - The new error type.
      Parameters:
      errorMapper - Used to map errors from type E to type U.
      Returns:
      Valid<U, T> when this is Valid, preserving the contained target T and the new error type binding U.
      New Invalid<U, T> when this is some Invalid. Containing errors converted to new type U, and preserving the target type T.
      Throws:
      X - If the errorMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If errorMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.mapError(CheckedFunction)
      • Accumulated.mapError(CheckedFunction)
      • Critical.mapError(CheckedFunction)

    • foldErrors

      <U, X extends Exception> Validation<U,T> foldErrors(CheckedFunction<? super List<E>, ? extends U, ? extends X> errorMapper) throws X
      Allows collapsing contained errors into a new type U, when this is Invalid. When this is Valid the fold is not applied as there are no errors, but the new error type binding U is preserved.

      The errorMapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null or empty errors as Accumulated.errors() and Critical.error() can never be null or empty.

      Checked exceptions thrown by errorMapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      U - The new error type.
      Parameters:
      errorMapper - Used to collapse errors into a new type U.
      Returns:
      Valid<U, T> when this is Valid, preserving the contained target T and the new error type binding U.
      New Invalid<U, T> when this is some Invalid. Containing error produced by errorMapper.
      Throws:
      X - If the errorMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If errorMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.foldErrors(CheckedFunction)
      • Accumulated.foldErrors(CheckedFunction)
      • Critical.foldErrors(CheckedFunction)

    • mapTarget

      <S, X extends Exception> Validation<E,S> mapTarget(CheckedFunction<? super T, ? extends S, ? extends X> mapper) throws X
      The functor (right biased) operation of Validation. Allows conversion of contained target from type T to type S, when this is Valid. When this is Invalid the conversion is not applied, but the new target type binding S is preserved.

      The mapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null as Valid.target() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by mapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      S - The new target type.
      Parameters:
      mapper - Used to map target from type T to type S.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, S> when this is Valid, containing the new target S and preserving the error type binding.
      Invalid<E, S> when this is some Invalid. Preserving contained errors, and binding the new target type S.
      Throws:
      X - If the mapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If mapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.mapTarget(CheckedFunction)
      • Invalid.mapTarget(CheckedFunction)

    • newTarget

      <S, X extends Exception> Validation<E,S> newTarget(CheckedSupplier<? extends S, ? extends X> targetSupplier) throws X
      Allows setting a new target S, when this is a Valid. When this is some Invalid the targetSupplier is not applied, but the new target type binding S is preserved.

      Checked exceptions thrown by targetSupplier are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Type Parameters:
      S - The new target type.
      Parameters:
      targetSupplier - Used to provide new target S.
      Returns:
      Valid<E, S> when this is Valid, containing the new target S and preserving the error type binding.
      Invalid<E, S> when this is some Invalid. Preserving contained errors, and binding the new target type S.
      Throws:
      X - If the targetSupplier throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If targetSupplier is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.newTarget(CheckedSupplier)
      • Invalid.newTarget(CheckedSupplier)

    • recover

      <X extends Exception> Validation<E,T> recover(CheckedFunction<? super List<E>, ? extends Validation<E,T>, ? extends X> mapper) throws X
      Allows recovery when this is some Invalid. Useful for recovering from a Critical, either converting it into a Accumulated or providing a new Valid.

      The mapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null or empty errors as Accumulated.errors() and Critical.error() can never be null or empty.

      Checked exceptions thrown by mapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Parameters:
      mapper - Used to consume potential errors and providing a new Validation<E, T>.
      Returns:
      this when this is Valid.
      mapper result when this is some Invalid.
      Throws:
      X - If the mapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If mapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.recover(CheckedFunction)
      • Invalid.recover(CheckedFunction)

    • isValid

      boolean isValid()
      Is this a Valid.
      Returns:
      true if this is a Valid, otherwise false
      See Also:
      • isInvalid()
      • isAccumulated()
      • isCritical()

    • isInvalid

      boolean isInvalid()
      Is this a Invalid.
      Returns:
      true if this is a Accumulated or Critical, otherwise false.
      See Also:
      • isValid()
      • isAccumulated()
      • isCritical()

    • isAccumulated

      boolean isAccumulated()
      Is this a Accumulated.
      Returns:
      true if this is a Accumulated, otherwise false
      See Also:
      • isValid()
      • isInvalid()
      • isCritical()

    • isCritical

      boolean isCritical()
      Is this a Critical.
      Returns:
      true if this is a Critical, otherwise false
      See Also:
      • isValid()
      • isInvalid()
      • isAccumulated()

    • toEither

      Either<List<E>,T> toEither()
      Projects this into an instance of Either.

      When this is a Valid, the result is a new Right containing the target value T. Otherwise, returns a new Left containing the errors List<E>.

      Either contains various utility functions that enable lazy reasoning about its contents.

      Returns:
      If this is a Valid, returns a Right<T>. Otherwise, returns a Left<List<E>>.
      See Also:
      • Either

    • get

      Option<T> get()
      Projects this into an instance of Option.

      When this is a Valid, the result is a new Present representation of Option, preserving the contained target value. Otherwise, returns a new Empty only preserving the type constraint T.

      Returns:
      If this is a Valid, returns a Present<T>. Otherwise, returns a Empty<T>.
      See Also:
      • Option
      • getErrors()

    • getErrors

      List<E> getErrors()
      Projects this into a List<E>.

      When this is a Invalid, the result is a new List<E> preserving the contained errors. Otherwise, returns an empty List<E>.

      Returns:
      List<E>, that is empty when this is Valid, or contains errors when this is Invalid.
      See Also:
      • get()
